--- title: "`{ospsuite}` Workflow" subtitle: "Introduction to `{ospsuite}`" module : "Introduction to `{ospsuite}`" ID: A02 date: last-modified institute: | ![](../resources/esqlabs.svg){height=100} format: esqlabs-revealjs --- ::: {.r-fit-text} ## **üéØ Goal: Get a general overview of the `{ospsuite}` workflow** ::: ::: {.cell} ::: # Introduction ## `{ospsuite}` An R package that supports A-Z PK-Sim and MoBi simulation workflow on **R**. - Load - Manipulate - Run - Visualize - Calculate PK-parameters - Perform (local) sensitivity analysis # R6 Reference Semantics ## R6 Reference Semantics {.scrollable} Most `{ospsuite}` objects (`Simulation`, `Parameter`, `Molecule`, `SimulationResults`, `DataCombined`, ...) are **R6**: reference semantics, not base-R copy-on-modify. - Base R (data frames, vectors, lists): **copy on modify** - R6 objects: **mutate in place**; `x <- y` creates an alias, not a copy ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite") sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath) a <- getParameter("Organism|Liver|Interstitial|Volume", sim) b <- a # NOT a copy ‚Äî same object setParameterValues(b, 1.2) a$value # 1.2 ‚Äî mutation of b visible on a ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 1.2 ``` ::: ::: Need fresh state? **Reload** the `.pkml` ‚Äî `{ospsuite}` R6 objects are not cloneable. ## Working With R6 Objects Access fields/methods with `$`; construct with `ClassName$new()`: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} sim$solver sim$outputSelections dc <- DataCombined$new() dc$addSimulationResults( simulationResults = simulationResults, quantitiesOrPaths = outputPath ) ``` ::: Practical rules: - `setParameterValues()`, `addOutputs()`, `clearOutputs()`, `addOutputInterval()` **mutate** their argument ‚Äî no reassignment - Passing an R6 to a function = passing a pointer; the function can change it - `reset()` on a parameter restores its pkml default without reloading - Fresh independent state: call `loadSimulation(path)` again # Basic workflow ## Load ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite") sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath) print(sim) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ‚Ä¢ Name: Vergin 1995 IV ‚Ä¢ Source file: D:/GitHub repositories/ESQlabs/PAGE2026-R-WORKSHOP/renv/library/windows/R-4.6/x86_64-w64-mingw32/ospsuite/extdata/Aciclovir.pkml ``` ::: ::: ## Manipulate {.scrollable} ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} parameterPath <- "Organism|Liver|Interstitial|Volume" param <- getParameter(parameterPath, sim) print(param) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ‚Ä¢ Quantity Type: Parameter ‚Ä¢ Path: Organism|Liver|Interstitial|Volume ‚Ä¢ Value: 0.35 [l] ‚Ä¢ Value Origin: Publication-Willmann S, Lippert J, Sevestre M, Solodenko J, Fois F, Schmitt W. PK-Sim¬Æ: a physiologically based pharmacokinetic ‚Äòwhole-body‚Äô model. Biosilico. 2003; 1 (4): 121-124. http://dx.doi.org/10.1016/S1478-5382%2803%2902342-4 ‚îÄ‚îÄ Formula ‚îÄ‚îÄ ‚Ä¢ isExplicit: TRUE ‚Ä¢ formula: f_int*V ‚Ä¢ Value overrides formula: FALSE ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} setParameterValues(param, 0.5) print(param) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ‚Ä¢ Quantity Type: Parameter ‚Ä¢ Path: Organism|Liver|Interstitial|Volume ‚Ä¢ Value: 0.50 [l] ‚Ä¢ Value Origin: Publication-Willmann S, Lippert J, Sevestre M, Solodenko J, Fois F, Schmitt W. PK-Sim¬Æ: a physiologically based pharmacokinetic ‚Äòwhole-body‚Äô model. Biosilico. 2003; 1 (4): 121-124. http://dx.doi.org/10.1016/S1478-5382%2803%2902342-4 ‚îÄ‚îÄ Formula ‚îÄ‚îÄ ‚Ä¢ isExplicit: TRUE ‚Ä¢ formula: f_int*V ‚Ä¢ Value overrides formula: TRUE ``` ::: ::: # R6 Reference Semantics ## Why This Matters Most `{ospsuite}` objects (`Simulation`, `Parameter`, `Molecule`, `SimulationResults`, `DataCombined`, ...) are **R6** objects. R6 behaves differently from base R: - Base R data frames, vectors, lists: **copy on modify** (functional style). - R6 objects: **reference semantics** (like Python, Java). Notice what happened on the previous slide: `setParameterValues(param, 0.5)` did not return a new parameter. We never reassigned `param`. Yet the next `print(param)` shows the new value. The object was **mutated in place**. ## Assignment Does Not Copy ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} a <- getParameter("Organism|Liver|Interstitial|Volume", sim) b <- a # NOT a copy ‚Äî b and a point to the same object setParameterValues(b, 1.2) a$value # also 1.2 ‚Äî mutating b mutates a ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 1.2 ``` ::: ::: Same applies to `sim`: `sim2 <- sim` gives two handles to one simulation. To work on an independent copy, **reload** the `.pkml`: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} sim_independent <- loadSimulation(simFilePath) # sim and sim_independent are now fully separate ``` ::: ## Fields, Methods, and `$new()` Access state and behavior with `$`: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} sim$solver # field ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ‚Ä¢ useJacobian: TRUE ‚Ä¢ h0: 1e-10 ‚Ä¢ hMin: 0 ‚Ä¢ hMax: 60 ‚Ä¢ mxStep: 100000 ‚Ä¢ relTol: 1e-05 ‚Ä¢ absTol: 1e-10 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} sim$outputSelections # field ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ‚Ä¢ Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood): Drug, Observer ‚Ä¢ Organism|VenousBlood|Plasma|Aciclovir|Plasma Unbound: Drug, Observer ``` ::: ::: Construct R6 objects with `ClassName$new(...)`: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} dc <- DataCombined$new() sa <- SensitivityAnalysis$new( simulation = sim, parameterPaths = "Aciclovir|Lipophilicity" ) ``` ::: Some methods are called via `$`: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} dc$addSimulationResults( simulationResults = simulationResults, quantitiesOrPaths = outputPath ) ``` ::: ## Practical Rules - Functions like `setParameterValues()`, `addOutputs()`, `clearOutputs()`, `addOutputInterval()` **mutate** their argument. No reassignment needed. - Passing an R6 object to a function is like passing a pointer ‚Äî the function can change it. - `x <- y` where `y` is R6: `x` is an alias, not a copy. - Need a fresh state? Call `loadSimulation(path)` again ‚Äî R6 objects in `{ospsuite}` are not cloneable. - `reset()` on a parameter restores its pkml default without reloading the whole simulation. # Basic workflow ## Run ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} # Always returns a named list simulationResults <- runSimulations(sim)[[1]] print(simulationResults) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ‚Ä¢ Number of individuals: 1 For paths: ‚Ä¢ Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) ‚Ä¢ Organism|VenousBlood|Plasma|Aciclovir|Plasma Unbound ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} # Convert simulation results to a data frame head(simulationResultsToDataFrame(simulationResults)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: ::: ## Visualize ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} # Detailed description in following slides plotTimeProfile(plotData = myDataCombined) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](A2-ospsuiteR_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-13-1.png){fig-align='center' width=61.7%} ::: ::: ## Calculate PK-parameters {.scrollable} ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} pkAnalyses <- calculatePKAnalyses(results = simulationResults) head(pkAnalysesToDataFrame(pkAnalyses)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: ::: ## Perform sensitivity analysis {.scrollable} ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} sa <- SensitivityAnalysis$new( simulation = sim, parameterPaths = c( "Aciclovir|Lipophilicity", "Events|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose" ) ) saResult <- runSensitivityAnalysis(sa) print(saResult) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ‚Ä¢ Number of calculated sensitivities: 44 Available PK Parameters: ‚Ä¢ C_max ‚Ä¢ t_max ‚Ä¢ C_tEnd ‚Ä¢ AUC_tEnd ‚Ä¢ AUC_inf ‚Ä¢ MRT ‚Ä¢ Thalf ‚Ä¢ FractionAucLastToInf ‚Ä¢ CL ‚Ä¢ Vss ‚Ä¢ Vd For the following outputs: ‚Ä¢ Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) ‚Ä¢ Organism|VenousBlood|Plasma|Aciclovir|Plasma Unbound ``` ::: ::: ## Resources - [`{ospsuite}` vignettes](https://www.open-systems-pharmacology.org/OSPSuite-R/articles/)

include-after-body:
<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
